checks.describe: core-internals profile is up: kind context kind-kubeadv-core answers and the worker node exists
API server is emitting audit events to stdout — the AUDIT=1 profile flag took effect
checks.run: kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core -n kube-system logs kube-apiserver-kubeadv-core-control-plane --tail=2000 | grep audit.k8s.io/v1 | head -1

checks.describe: bounded object set seeded: 300 (8 GB) or 600 (16 GB) module=m2 ConfigMaps in the apiload namespace
checks.run: kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core -n apiload get configmaps -l module=m2 --no-headers | wc -l | tr -d ' \n'

checks.describe: FlowSchema nimbusai-critical exists and routes to the nimbusai-critical priority level
PriorityLevelConfiguration nimbusai-critical exists as a Limited level that queues (not rejects) overflow
checks.run: kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get prioritylevelconfiguration nimbusai-critical -o jsonpath='{.spec.type} {.spec.limited.limitResponse.type}'

checks.describe: the protected ServiceAccount nimbusai-critical exists in the nimbusai namespace
checks.run: kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core -n nimbusai get serviceaccount nimbusai-critical -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'

checks.describe: APF dispatch counters are exposed and the storm's global-default traffic is visible in /metrics
checks.run: kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_flowcontrol_dispatched_requests_total | grep global-default | head -1

checks.describe: the nimbusai-critical FlowSchema actually classified requests — its dispatch counter exists
checks.run: kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_flowcontrol_dispatched_requests_total | grep nimbusai-critical | head -1
